items.title: Conditional Approval Request
items.transcript_prompt: Listen to the question and choose the best response.

audio_script.question: If the factory yield stabilization review is not cleared by May 19, can we still release the client summary?

responses.A: The summary was printed in the training room yesterday.
responses.B: I have not reserved the larger conference room yet.
responses.C: Only if Legal signs off on the exception before the summary is circulated.

items.prompt_text: Listen to the question and choose the best response.

options.A: The summary was printed in the training room yesterday.
options.B: I have not reserved the larger conference room yet.
options.C: Only if Legal signs off on the exception before the summary is circulated.

items.explanation: The best response recognizes the conditional dependency and gives a constrained, professionally hedged answer rather than ignoring the approval risk. Option C is therefore the most appropriate response.

items.title: Vendor Status Escalation
items.transcript_prompt: Listen to the question and choose the best response.

audio_script.question: Would it be premature to escalate a contract clause whose wording conflicts with the latest portal notice to the steering committee?

responses.A: Not if the vendor misses the revised checkpoint this afternoon.
responses.B: The committee usually meets in the west conference room.
responses.C: I can send you the travel itinerary after lunch.

items.prompt_text: Listen to the question and choose the best response.

options.A: Not if the vendor misses the revised checkpoint this afternoon.
options.B: The committee usually meets in the west conference room.
options.C: I can send you the travel itinerary after lunch.

items.explanation: The correct option handles the negative question pragmatically and ties escalation to a measurable trigger. Option A is therefore the most appropriate response.

audio_script.question: Do you think Finance will object to reallocating $42,750 from the contingency line?

responses.C: The client lunch was moved to Thursday.

items.title: Policy Interpretation
items.transcript_prompt: Listen to the question and choose the best response.

audio_script.question: The portal notice and the signed policy seem to conflict; which one should we rely on for now?

responses.A: The portal password expires every ninety days.
responses.B: I thought the cafeteria was closed for renovations.
responses.C: Use the signed policy until Compliance confirms that the notice supersedes it.

items.prompt_text: Listen to the question and choose the best response.

options.A: The portal password expires every ninety days.
options.B: I thought the cafeteria was closed for renovations.
options.C: Use the signed policy until Compliance confirms that the notice supersedes it.

items.explanation: The response resolves a document hierarchy issue without overclaiming authority. Option C is therefore the most appropriate response.

responses.B: The audit trail is stored in the old lobby cabinet.
responses.C: Testing windows are always larger on the south side.
